seo.title: Amazon Bedrock Agents Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for Amazon Bedrock Agents agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams deploying Amazon Bedrock Agents face heightened compliance risks due to dynamic customer interactions involving product recommendations, pricing, and personalized offers. Without robust guardrails, agents can generate unsubstantiated claims, misrepresent pricing, or mishandle sensitive customer data, violating FTC guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, and PCI DSS. Vex provides post-processing verification for invoke_agent() responses to ensure regulatory compliance and prevent costly enforcement actions.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims must be substantiated with evidence; agents cannot make deceptive statements about product benefits or availability.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies agent responses against knowledge base documents to block unsubstantiated claims before customer delivery.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data handling requires explicit consent and data minimization; agents must not retain or share PII without authorization.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans responses for PII leakage and enforces data retention policies across multi-turn conversations.

complianceRequirements.description: Payment card data must never be processed or stored by agents; all transactions must redirect to secure PCI-compliant endpoints.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex blocks responses containing card numbers or CVVs and validates payment flow redirection.

complianceRequirements.description: Agent responses must be accessible, avoiding complex language or assumptions about user abilities.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex ensures responses use clear, readable language and provide alternative formats for accessibility.

industryRisks.name: Unsubstantiated Product Claims
industryRisks.description: Agent recommends products with exaggerated benefits not supported by knowledge base documents.
industryRisks.example: Agent claims 'Our moisturizer eliminates wrinkles in 24 hours' without clinical evidence.

industryRisks.name: Pricing Misrepresentation
industryRisks.description: Dynamic pricing changes not reflected accurately in agent responses lead to disputes.
industryRisks.example: Agent quotes $49.99 for shoes that checkout at $69.99 due to session state loss.

industryRisks.name: PII Data Leakage
industryRisks.description: Knowledge base retrieval exposes customer data inappropriately in multi-turn conversations.
industryRisks.example: Agent references previous purchase 'John Doe's credit card ending 1234' without consent.

industryRisks.name: Inaccurate Refund Policy
industryRisks.description: Agent provides outdated or incorrect return policy information from malformed Lambda responses.
industryRisks.example: Agent states '30-day returns' when policy changed to 14 days.

implementation.title: Verify Product Claims Against Knowledge Base
implementation.description: Wrap Bedrock agent invocation to validate claims match retrieved KB documents using Vex policy.
implementation.code:
import boto3
import vex

bedrock = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key='your-vex-api-key')

response = bedrock.invoke_agent(
    agentId='YOUR_AGENT_ID',
    agentAliasId='YOUR_ALIAS_ID',
    sessionId='session-123',
    inputText='Recommend moisturizers for dry skin.'
)

completion = response['completion']

policy_result = vex_client.verify(
    input=completion,
    policy='ecommerce-product-claims',
    context={'kb_documents': response.get('retrievedDocuments', []), 'category': 'skincare'}
)

if policy_result.status == 'pass':
    print(completion)
else:
    print('Blocked: Unsubstantiated claim detected')

implementation.title: Block PII and Pricing Violations
implementation.description: Post-process agent responses to catch pricing errors and PII before customer sees them.

implementation.title: TypeScript Integration for Lambda Action Groups
implementation.description: Verify Lambda action group responses in Node.js before returning to Bedrock Agent.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Amazon Bedrock Agents in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
BeautyCo, a mid-sized cosmetics retailer, deployed Amazon Bedrock Agents to handle 80% of customer service interactions including product recommendations, pricing inquiries, and returns processing. The agent integrated with their Shopify knowledge base containing 50K+ product documents and Lambda action groups for inventory checks and order management. During Black Friday, the agent handled 10K+ sessions daily, but issues emerged: knowledge base chunking missed pricing updates, Lambda functions returned malformed JSON causing 15% session state loss, and guardrails failed to catch FTC-violating claims like 'Our serum guarantees wrinkle reduction in 7 days' when KB evidence only supported 'may improve appearance.'

This led to 300+ customer complaints, $50K in refund disputes from pricing mismatches, and FTC scrutiny after viral social media posts. Without compliance guardrails, BeautyCo faced potential 7-figure fines and brand damage.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After implementing Vex verification on invoke_agent() responses, BeautyCo blocked 98% of compliance violations pre-delivery. The Python SDK wrapper caught unsubstantiated claims by cross-referencing KB retrievals, TypeScript Lambda validation enforced pricing accuracy, and PII policies prevented data leaks across multi-turn sessions. Compliance violations dropped to <0.5%, customer satisfaction rose 22%, and refund disputes fell 87%, saving $42K monthly while avoiding regulatory action.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex guardrails into your Bedrock Agents today to eliminate compliance risks and protect revenue.
